title: OtpInvalid
description: Event: `otp_invalid`. Emitted when the caller's OTP attempt is rejected — wrong code, expired, max attempts reached, or record not found. When `attemptsRemaining` is 0 the session is locked; the client must restart the OTP flow. When greater than 0 the client may prompt the user to try again.

requestId.description: Echoes the `requestId` from the originating `verify_otp` action.
The request ID this verification belongs to.

error.description: Machine-readable failure reason. Absent if the server cannot determine a specific cause.

attemptsRemaining.description: How many more attempts the caller has before the OTP is locked. Zero means the session is locked and a new OTP must be requested.

message.description: Human-readable failure message suitable for display in the verification UI.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the event was emitted.
